title: Enforce the Harness workflow at runtime
goal: Replace skill-led requirements, planning, approval, delegation, execution, and verification with a Pi-internal, evidence-based Harness state machine.

acceptanceCriteria.description: hrn loads no workflow skills, rejects non-interactive workflow execution, and uses a guardian as its only workflow-state writer.
A durable ordered refinement, domain-modeling, and shared-understanding gate precedes planning.
Only a structurally verifiable plan and a specific interactive user approval can permit execution.
Structured, role-limited delegation with recorded evidence is required before a subagent may run.
Pi-mediated mutation, shell, workflow-artifact, and delegation access is phase- and work-unit-gated.
The TUI shows the current phase below the editor and a prioritized five-line-or-fewer work list above it.
Architecture documentation and regression, package, and repository verification cover the new contract.
Release automation, Homebrew synchronization, documentation, and package identity agree on the current package.
Parent-only guardian transitions, committed delegation bindings, CAS state writes, approval recovery, and risk reopening are enforced.
Child runtime compatibility, parent-reviewed evidence, and risk reopening are enforced.

workUnits.title: Implement v2 protocol and evidence model
Implement guardian transitions and tool authorization
Harden launcher resource and mode boundaries
Render workflow stage and prioritized work list
Add regression coverage and run repository verification
